main_paper.title: LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models
main_paper.abstract: LoRA freezes pretrained model weights and injects trainable low-rank matrices into transformer layers, reducing the number of trainable parameters for downstream adaptation while preserving model quality.

sections.title: Abstract
sections.text: LoRA adapts large pretrained language models by freezing the original weights and training rank-decomposition matrices. The paper claims strong task quality while reducing trainable parameters, GPU memory, and storage costs compared with full fine-tuning.

sections.title: Methodology
sections.text: The method inserts low-rank update matrices into selected transformer projections. Relative to adapters [1] and prefix tuning [2], LoRA does not add inference latency because the low-rank update can be merged into the base weight matrix after training.

sections.title: Evaluation
sections.text: Experiments report competitive or better results on language understanding and generation benchmarks compared with fine-tuning, adapters [1], prefix tuning [2], and GPT-3 style prompting [3]. The strongest claim is that LoRA reaches similar quality with far fewer trainable parameters.

sections.title: References
sections.text: [1] Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for NLP. [2] Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation. [3] Language Models are Few-Shot Learners. [4] Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization.

citations.title: Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for NLP
citations.context_snippet: Relative to adapters [1], LoRA does not add inference latency because the low-rank update can be merged into the base weight matrix.

citations.title: Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation
citations.context_snippet: LoRA compares against prefix tuning [2] as another parameter-efficient fine-tuning baseline.

citations.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
citations.context_snippet: LoRA evaluates against GPT-3 style prompting [3] to position fine-tuning against prompting baselines.

citations.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
citations.context_snippet: LoRA uses standard optimizer choices such as Adam [4], so replication needs optimizer settings.

claims.text: LoRA reduces trainable parameters by several orders of magnitude compared with full fine-tuning.
LoRA adds no inference latency after the low-rank updates are merged into model weights.
LoRA reaches competitive benchmark quality versus full fine-tuning and other parameter-efficient baselines.

paper.title: Parameter-Efficient Transfer Learning for NLP
paper.abstract: Adapters insert small trainable modules into each transformer layer while keeping most pretrained parameters frozen.

sections.title: Adapter Layers
sections.text: Adapter modules add bottleneck projections between transformer blocks, creating a compact trainable path for new tasks.

cit_adapter.summary: Relative to LoRA, adapter layers are the key baseline for parameter-efficient tuning. They support LoRA's motivation because they show frozen-backbone adaptation works, but they also sharpen LoRA's latency claim because adapter modules remain in the inference path.
cit_adapter.why_it_matters_for_main_paper: LoRA needs adapters as a baseline to prove that low-rank weight updates can keep the parameter-efficiency benefit while avoiding adapter inference overhead.
supporting_evidence: Adapter modules are trainable bottleneck layers.
LoRA compares against adapters when arguing for no additional inference latency.
cit_adapter.possible_contradiction: The latency comparison depends on implementation details and should be benchmarked under the same serving stack.

paper.title: Prefix-Tuning: Optimizing Continuous Prompts for Generation
paper.abstract: Prefix-tuning keeps language model parameters frozen and optimizes continuous prefix vectors for generation tasks.

cit_prefix.summary: Relative to LoRA, prefix tuning is another frozen-model adaptation baseline. It helps frame whether LoRA's low-rank updates are a better tradeoff than task-specific continuous prompts.
cit_prefix.why_it_matters_for_main_paper: The comparison helps judges see that LoRA is not just parameter-efficient, but also positioned against prompt-based adaptation.
supporting_evidence: Both methods freeze most pretrained weights.
LoRA reports comparisons against prefix tuning.

paper.title: Language Models are Few-Shot Learners
paper.abstract: GPT-3 demonstrates that very large language models can perform tasks from prompts with few or no gradient updates.

cit_gpt3.summary: Relative to LoRA, GPT-3 few-shot prompting contextualizes the alternative of adapting behavior through prompts instead of updating weights.
cit_gpt3.why_it_matters_for_main_paper: It clarifies why LoRA should be judged against both gradient-based adaptation and prompt-only baselines.

paper.title: Adam: A Method for Stochastic Optimization
paper.abstract: Adam is an adaptive stochastic optimization method widely used in neural network training.

cit_adam.summary: Relative to LoRA, Adam is an implementation dependency rather than a scientific baseline. It matters for replication because optimizer hyperparameters affect stability and quality.
cit_adam.why_it_matters_for_main_paper: A replication harness should capture optimizer choices before comparing LoRA against baselines.
supporting_evidence: Optimization details can shift fine-tuning outcomes.

critique_findings.title: Latency claim needs matched serving benchmark
critique_findings.body: LoRA's no-latency claim is plausible after merge, but the comparison to adapters should be measured under the same batching, hardware, and serving stack.

critique_findings.title: Rank sensitivity ablation should drive replication
critique_findings.body: The evaluation should stress how rank choice affects quality, memory, and speed across tasks. This is a concrete missing experiment for the Code and Replication agents.

code_repo.name: loralib
code_repo.description: Reference implementation for LoRA: Low-Rank Adaptation of Large Language Models.
code_repo.match_reason: Fixture match for the LoRA paper title and arXiv ID.

replication.claim_under_test: LoRA reaches adapter-level quality without adapter inference latency.
minimal_reproduction_steps: Install the reference repo in a clean Python environment.
Run the smallest published LoRA task configuration.
Run an adapter baseline under the same batch size and hardware.
Compare accuracy, trainable parameters, peak memory, and tokens/sec.
